title: Kajis findings
wikitext:
[]: Kaji's findings?             Quote Post by SkyFire360 » Sun Jan 29, 2006 1:36 am First off, let me say that this forum is absolutely wonderful. I've been searching for a place to discuss Evangelion, its symbolism and its meanings ever since I saw the series 8 years ago. I hope to contribute in any way I can to this forum! On to my topic... In the End of Evangelion (around 6:54 into epidode 25' [AIR]), Misato admits to discovering why the second impact truly happened. The information she found - presumably from the capsule that Kaji gave her - only flashes up on the screen for a few frames before being replaced by "DELETED". On the VHS tapes, the quality was low enough so that the information in Kaji's findings was not legible. However, with the release of the DVD version most if not all of the text on the entire page is legible. It starts off with "1. A group comprised primarily of college students living in and around Osaka got together to put on GEHIRN, the ADAM ???tion". At first glance, the text seems to be nonsense, but upon further inspection it seems like a hidden message may be present. Phrases like "Building on this experience, the core members of the con's staff opened 'SECOND IMPACT', an ADAM specialty shop, in SECOND IMPACT." Nonsense when read literally, but the occurance of SECOND IMPACT twice in the sentence in two unlikely places seems to be intentionally wrong. There is also a large block of binary code in the file, though I cannot seem to decode it in any logical form. There are five blocks of seven rows with ten columns, giving 350 binary digits. Unfortunately 350 is not divisible by 8 evenly, so a simple binary->ASCII conversion yields nothing. Does anyone else have any ideas on what this file may be referring to or what the binary code means? Image is as follows:

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 29, 2006 4:14 am The text is a history of GAINAX with various NGE names substituted - one of the 2nd Impact report documents we get a brief glimpse of is similar. As for the mysterious 350-bit string - I've never seen anyone attempt a cryptanalysis of it.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Jan 29, 2006 6:41 am Considering that there is a '4' amid all the ones and zeroes, I suspect this is something someone just typed up. The ten bit partitions also do not help analysis. Perhaps it's a bitmap?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Jan 29, 2006 10:46 am We see similar numbers all over the place in the show. Might be worth looking into hexadecimal or octal based binary as well. Maybe it's a simple reference to the digitalisation of DNA (Nerv would dig that sort of stuff and they like making computer copies of souls, so that would fit). When we see later in the movie the 3rd Impact data being compared from 15 years ago... I thought it was Adam's DNA mutation expressed digitally. I also thought the Dead Sea Scrolls were literally Adam's DNA decoded into hebrew or whatever. But that's not worth looking into anyway, as many would say. The language of DNA is digital, but not binary. Where binary encoding has 0 and 1 to work with, DNA has 4 positions, T, C, G and A. A digital byte is mostly 8 binary digits, a DNA 'byte' (called a codon) has three digits. Because each digit can have 4 values instead of 2, an DNA codon has 64 ( 1 ) possible values, compared to a binary byte which has 256 ( 2 ) . So trying to decode it like that would get messy. I tried putting that into Paint BTW (the 0's being white pixels and the 1's being black pixels) but couldnâ€™t see much, maybe it is written in kanji though, so I wouldnâ€™t be able to recognise it anyway. So, uh... yeah. I'm gonna say it's not worth looking into too much, unless you want to blow your mind. ( 1 ) I Ching again... those bastards. ( 2 ) A rainbow! Edit: It's funny that the data Misato was viewing was deleted at 6:00AM. Which book said that on the last day, or the end of the world, would start as soon as the sun warmed the face of man?

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sun Jan 29, 2006 12:52 pm It may sound stupid, but I think its just a joke made by some creative and humorous people. Its a great idea, to be honest... lol...

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Jan 29, 2006 1:16 pm FauÃŸtin wrote: It may sound stupid, but I think its just a joke made by some creative and humorous people. Its a great idea, to be honest... lol...

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sun Jan 29, 2006 1:43 pm ZOMG!!!111oneoneone, those binary sequences are obvious refrences to the angels! :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 29, 2006 2:03 pm SEELE 08 wrote: ZOMG!!!111oneoneone, those binary sequences are obvious refrences to the angels! :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Jan 29, 2006 3:17 pm this is pretty funny, it says Gehirn made Top O nerae!

[]:             Quote Post by K2Grey » Sun Jan 29, 2006 4:45 pm Now, someone make a NERV logo with "Aim for the Top!" as the motto.

[]:             Quote Post by Phoenix Feet » Sun Jan 29, 2006 6:41 pm I tried chucking it through a binary/hexadecimal translator, just gave me jibberish. Like the other guys said, it's probably not worth taking something that shows up on an in-joke to seriously.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sun Jan 29, 2006 7:30 pm Apparently SEELE sponsered some kind of amateur events.
